## [00:00 - 03:05]

Hi, this is Don Crowther. Let's talk about your pre-registration email strategies. This is the set of emails that you're going to send out before your webinar to invite people to register for your webinar. Now the ideal situation is that this is not cold traffic. The ideal situation is that you have a relationship with these people before you ever invite them to register for a webinar. In other words, they're on your list and you've sent them some emails to introduce yourself, to create value, to cause them to want to attend your webinar. We'll talk separately about ad traffic, about affiliate traffic, and how to handle that. But this is the ideal situation. You want to have met the girl before you ask her to go out on a date with you. The best pre-registration strategy starts a week before you ever open up the webinar registration page. In other words, you're actually sending them emails about the subject of your webinar a week before you ever even start talking about the webinar. So this is a series of emails sent the week before the webinar registration is offered in which you set up the problem the webinar will address and start talking about solutions to that problem, all without ever even mentioning the webinar. I recommend two to three emails during this period that do what we just talked about. When you do this, basically announcing the webinar is then a natural extension of the process, something like this. Over the last week, I sent you several emails talking about blank and how to solve it. From the emails and feedback I've received, it's clear that this is an issue on many people's mind. I suspect that you're one of them. Now I'm going to take a side journey here for a moment and give you a bonus tip. Notice in that line, I'm writing to one person, not to the group. So I suspect you're one of them, not. It's clear that this is something that my audience is interested in. So here's your side journey for a moment. People want you to talk to them as a person, not as part of a group. And everything you do in email, in all of your email marketing, don't talk to your group. Talk to an individual, each individual person within that group.

## [15:11 - 15:18]

But in most cases, you're not mentioning anything about your product. It's all about why they should be attending your webinar.
